Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Everglades Corporation (Fka Everglades Steamship Corporation)
Off Calendar May-24-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Everglades Corporation (fka Everglades Steamship Corporation).
Dept. 514. Judge: Cynthia Ming-Mei Lee. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

James H. Ghilotti, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Herbert Schmeltz, who was not present at this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defaulted defendant Everglades Corporation (fka Everglades Steamship Corporation).
Plaintiff's first amended complaint was filed on October 31, 2013 and served on defendant Everglades Corporation (fka Everglades Steamship Corporation) on June 11, 2014.
Default was entered against defendant Everglades Corporation (fka Everglades Steamship Corporation) August 19, 2014.
Statement of Damages was served on June 11, 2014 and filed on August 19, 2014.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on April 2, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Herbert Schmeltz, and against the Defaulted Defendant Everglades Corporation (fka Everglades Steamship Corporation), as follows:

Economic Damages - $50,000.00.
Non-Economic Damages - $300,000.00.
Total - $350,000.00.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Roger D. Yuen of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / moving party Flowserve US Inc. as sucessor-in-interest to Durametallic Corporation
Patrick R. Campbell of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Herbert Schmeltz

RULING:
DEFENDANT FLOWSERVE US, INC. AS SUCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO DURAMETALLIC CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Flowserve US, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of submitting evidence to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claim. As to identification of Durametallic packing, when read together, plaintiff's deposition testimony and Mr. Smith's declaration create a triable issue whether the packing at issue was Durametallic packing. As to asbestos content, plaintiff's deposition testimony and Mr. Smith's declaration, the only evidence defendant relied upon on this topic, are insufficient to sustain defendant's burden. Plaintiff's objections to Mr. Smith's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Early K. Langley of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #3537 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)
